children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Bill Emerson National Hunger Fellows and Mickey Leland International Hunger Fellows Program Act of 2008”.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 1 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the head of the Congressional Hunger Center.

children.label: A
children.text: a Bill Emerson Hunger Fellow; or

children.label: B
children.text: Mickey Leland Hunger Fellow.

children.heading: Fellowship Programs
children.label: 3 Fellowship Programs
children.text: The term “Fellowship Programs” means the Bill Emerson National Hunger Fellowship Program and the Mickey Leland International Hunger Fellowship Program established under subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established the Bill Emerson National Hunger Fellowship Program and the Mickey Leland International Hunger Fellowship Program.

children.label: I
children.text: to pursue careers in humanitarian and public service;

children.label: II
children.text: to recognize the needs of low-income people and hungry people;

children.label: III
children.text: to provide assistance to people in need; and

children.label: IV
children.text: to seek public policy solutions to the challenges of hunger and poverty;

children.label: ii
children.text: to provide training and development opportunities for such leaders through placement in programs operated by appropriate organizations or entities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to increase awareness of the importance of public service.

children.heading: Bill Emerson Hunger Fellowship Program
children.label: B Bill Emerson Hunger Fellowship Program
children.text: The purpose of the Bill Emerson Hunger Fellowship Program is to address hunger and poverty in the United States.

children.heading: Mickey Leland Hunger Fellowship Program
children.label: C Mickey Leland Hunger Fellowship Program
children.text: The purpose of the Mickey Leland Hunger Fellowship Program is to address international hunger and other humanitarian needs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall offer to provide a grant to the Congressional Hunger Center to administer the Fellowship Programs.

children.heading: Terms of grant
children.label: B Terms of grant
children.text: The terms of the grant provided under subparagraph (A), including the length of the grant and provisions for the alteration or termination of the grant, shall be determined by the Secretary in accordance with this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall make available Bill Emerson Hunger Fellowships and Mickey Leland Hunger Fellowships in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: training in direct service programs for the hungry and other anti-hunger programs in conjunction with community-based organizations through a program of field placement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: providing experience in policy development through placement in a governmental entity or nongovernmental, nonprofit, or private sector organization.

children.heading: Work plan
children.label: B Work plan
children.text: To carry out subparagraph (A) and assist in the evaluation of the fellowships under paragraph (6), the Director shall, for each fellow, approve a work plan that identifies the target objectives for the fellow in the fellowship, including specific duties and responsibilities relating to those objectives.

children.heading: Bill Emerson Hunger Fellow
children.label: A Bill Emerson Hunger Fellow
children.text: A Bill Emerson Hunger Fellowship awarded under this section shall be for not more than 15 months.

children.heading: Mickey Leland Hunger Fellow
children.label: B Mickey Leland Hunger Fellow
children.text: A Mickey Leland Hunger Fellowship awarded under this section shall be for not more than 2 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Fellowships shall be awarded pursuant to a nationwide competition established by the Director.

children.label: i
children.text: an intent to pursue a career in humanitarian services and outstanding potential for such a career;

children.label: ii
children.text: leadership potential or actual leadership experience;

children.label: iii
children.text: diverse life experience;

children.label: iv
children.text: proficient writing and speaking skills;

children.label: v
children.text: an ability to live in poor or diverse communities; and

children.label: vi
children.text: such other attributes as are considered to be appropriate by the Director.

children.label: i
children.text: a living allowance during the term of the Fellowship; and

children.label: ii
children.text: subject to subparagraph (B), an end-of-service award.

children.heading: Requirement for successful completion of fellowship
children.label: B Requirement for successful completion of fellowship
children.text: Each fellow shall be entitled to receive an end-of-service award at an appropriate rate for each month of satisfactory service completed, as determined by the Director.

children.label: i
children.text: the Department of Agriculture;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Congressional Hunger Center; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a host agency in the field or policy placement of the fellow.

children.heading: Emerson Fellow
children.label: i Emerson Fellow
children.text: An individual awarded a fellowship from the Bill Emerson Hunger Fellowship shall be known as an “Emerson Fellow”.

children.heading: Leland Fellow
children.label: ii Leland Fellow
children.text: An individual awarded a fellowship from the Mickey Leland Hunger Fellowship shall be known as a “Leland Fellow”.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct periodic evaluations of the Fellowship Programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: arrange for annual independent financial audits of expenditures under the Fellowship Programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), in carrying out this section, the Director may solicit, accept, use, and dispose of gifts, bequests, or devises of services or property, both real and personal, for the purpose of facilitating the work of the Fellowship Programs.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: Gifts, bequests, or devises of money and proceeds from sales of other property received as gifts, bequests, or devises shall be used exclusively for the purposes of the Fellowship Programs.
describes the activities and expenditures of the Fellowship Programs during the preceding fiscal year, including expenditures made from funds made available under subsection (g); and
includes the results of evaluations and audits required by subsection (d).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary such sums as are necessary to carry out this section, to remain available until expended.
